---
name: Bug Report
about: Report a bug with battery detection, the overlay, or app behavior in Headset
  Battery Widget
title: "[BUG]"
labels: ''
assignees: ''

---

**Describe the bug**
A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Open Headset Battery Widget
2. ...
3. See error

**Expected behavior**
What you expected the widget to show or do.

**Actual behavior**
What it showed or did instead. If the widget reads `--%`, include the status text shown below the percentage (e.g. "No headset device detected", "Headset found but not active", "Battery N/A").

**Screenshots**
If applicable, add screenshots of the widget and/or the Diagnostics window.

**Headset**
 - Model: [e.g. HyperX Cloud II Wireless, Kingston Cloud II Wireless, Cloud Stinger 2 Wireless, Cloud Alpha Wireless]
 - Powered on at the time of the bug: [yes / no]
 - Connected via: [wireless USB dongle / USB cable charging only]
 - Approximate battery level shown by the headset itself (LED, ear cup beep, official software): [e.g. ~50%, unknown]

**Diagnostics output**
Open the widget, right-click it (or right-click the tray icon) -> Diagnostics. Copy or screenshot the row for your headset. Include all columns: VID, PID, Usage, UsagePage, Manufacturer, Product.

**System**
 - Windows edition: [e.g. Windows 11 Pro]
 - Windows build: [run `winver` and paste the build number, e.g. 26100.2152]
 - Widget version: [e.g. 1.7.0.0 - check Settings > Apps > Installed apps > Headset Battery Widget]
 - Install source: [Microsoft Store / sideloaded MSIX]

**Additional context**
Anything else that might be relevant: other headset software running (NGENUITY, HyperX Ngenuity, Kingston software, SteelSeries GG, etc.), recent Windows updates, USB hub vs direct port, etc.
